1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	
3	"""
4	🦥 Starter Script for Fine-Tuning FastLanguageModel with Unsloth
5	
6	This script is designed as a starting point for fine-tuning your models using unsloth.
7	It includes configurable options for model loading, PEFT parameters, training arguments, 
8	and model saving/pushing functionalities.
9	
10	You will likely want to customize this script to suit your specific use case 
11	and requirements.
12	
13	Here are a few suggestions for customization:
14	    - Modify the dataset loading and preprocessing steps to match your data.
15	    - Customize the model saving and pushing configurations.
16	
17	Usage: (most of the options have valid default values this is an extended example for demonstration purposes)
18	    python unsloth-cli.py --model_name "unsloth/llama-3-8b" --max_seq_length 8192 --dtype None --load_in_4bit \
19	    --r 64 --lora_alpha 32 --lora_dropout 0.1 --bias "none" --use_gradient_checkpointing "unsloth" \
20	    --random_state 3407 --use_rslora --per_device_train_batch_size 4 --gradient_accumulation_steps 8 \
21	    --warmup_steps 5 --max_steps 400 --learning_rate 2e-6 --logging_steps 1 --optim "adamw_8bit" \
22	    --weight_decay 0.005 --lr_scheduler_type "linear" --seed 3407 --output_dir "outputs" \
23	    --report_to "tensorboard" --save_model --save_path "model" --quantization_method "f16" \
24	    --push_model --hub_path "hf/model" --hub_token "your_hf_token"
25	
26	To see a full list of configurable options, use:
27	    python unsloth-cli.py --help
28	
29	Happy fine-tuning!
30	"""
31	
32	import argparse
33	import os
34	
35	
36	def run(args):
37	    import torch
38	    from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
39	    from datasets import load_dataset
40	    from transformers.utils import strtobool
41	    from trl import SFTTrainer, SFTConfig
42	    from transformers import TrainingArguments
43	    from unsloth import is_bfloat16_supported
44	    import logging
45	    logging.getLogger('hf-to-gguf').setLevel(logging.WARNING)
46	
47	    # Load model and tokenizer
48	    model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
49	        model_name=args.model_name,
50	        max_seq_length=args.max_seq_length,
51	        dtype=args.dtype,
52	        load_in_4bit=args.load_in_4bit,
53	    )
54	
55	    # Configure PEFT model
56	    model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
57	        model,
58	        r=args.r,
59	        target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
60	                        "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
61	        lora_alpha=args.lora_alpha,
62	        lora_dropout=args.lora_dropout,
63	        bias=args.bias,
64	        use_gradient_checkpointing=args.use_gradient_checkpointing,
65	        random_state=args.random_state,
66	        use_rslora=args.use_rslora,
67	        loftq_config=args.loftq_config,
68	    )
69	
70	    alpaca_prompt = """Below is an instruction that describes a task, paired with an input that provides further context. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.
71	
72	    ### Instruction:
73	    {}
74	
75	    ### Input:
76	    {}
77	
78	    ### Response:
79	    {}"""
80	
81	    EOS_TOKEN = tokenizer.eos_token  # Must add EOS_TOKEN
82	    def formatting_prompts_func(examples):
83	        instructions = examples["instruction"]
84	        inputs       = examples["input"]
85	        outputs      = examples["output"]
86	        texts = []
87	        for instruction, input, output in zip(instructions, inputs, outputs):
88	            text = alpaca_prompt.format(instruction, input, output) + EOS_TOKEN
89	            texts.append(text)
90	        return {"text": texts}
91	
92	    use_modelscope = strtobool(os.environ.get('UNSLOTH_USE_MODELSCOPE', 'False'))
93	    if use_modelscope:
94	        from modelscope import MsDataset
95	        dataset = MsDataset.load(args.dataset, split="train")
96	    else:
97	        # Load and format dataset
98	        dataset = load_dataset(args.dataset, split="train")
99	    dataset = dataset.map(formatting_prompts_func, batched=True)
100	    print("Data is formatted and ready!")
101	
102	    # Configure training arguments
103	    training_args = SFTConfig(
104	        per_device_train_batch_size=args.per_device_train_batch_size,
105	        gradient_accumulation_steps=args.gradient_accumulation_steps,
106	        warmup_steps=args.warmup_steps,
107	        max_steps=args.max_steps,
108	        learning_rate=args.learning_rate,
109	        fp16=not is_bfloat16_supported(),
110	        bf16=is_bfloat16_supported(),
111	        logging_steps=args.logging_steps,
112	        optim=args.optim,
113	        weight_decay=args.weight_decay,
114	        lr_scheduler_type=args.lr_scheduler_type,
115	        seed=args.seed,
116	        output_dir=args.output_dir,
117	        report_to=args.report_to,
118	        max_length=args.max_seq_length,
119	        dataset_num_proc=2,
120	        packing=False,
121	    )
122	
123	    # Initialize trainer
124	    trainer = SFTTrainer(
125	        model=model,
126	        processing_class=tokenizer,
127	        train_dataset=dataset,
128	        args=training_args,
129	    )
130	
131	    # Train model
132	    trainer_stats = trainer.train()
133	
134	    # Save model
135	    if args.save_model:
136	        # if args.quantization_method is a list, we will save the model for each quantization method
137	        if args.save_gguf:
138	            if isinstance(args.quantization, list):
139	                for quantization_method in args.quantization:
140	                    print(f"Saving model with quantization method: {quantization_method}")
141	                    model.save_pretrained_gguf(
142	                        args.save_path,
143	                        tokenizer,
144	                        quantization_method=quantization_method,
145	                    )
146	                    if args.push_model:
147	                        model.push_to_hub_gguf(
148	                            hub_path=args.hub_path,
149	                            hub_token=args.hub_token,
150	                            quantization_method=quantization_method,
151	                        )
152	            else:
153	                print(f"Saving model with quantization method: {args.quantization}")
154	                model.save_pretrained_gguf(args.save_path, tokenizer, quantization_method=args.quantization)
155	                if args.push_model:
156	                    model.push_to_hub_gguf(
157	                        hub_path=args.hub_path,
158	                        hub_token=args.hub_token,
159	                        quantization_method=quantization_method,
160	                    )
161	        else:
162	            model.save_pretrained_merged(args.save_path, tokenizer, args.save_method)
163	            if args.push_model:
164	                model.push_to_hub_merged(args.save_path, tokenizer, args.hub_token)
165	    else:
166	        print("Warning: The model is not saved!")
167	
168	
169	if __name__ == "__main__":
170	
171	    # Define argument parser
172	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="🦥 Fine-tune your llm faster using unsloth!")
173	
174	    model_group = parser.add_argument_group("🤖 Model Options")
175	    model_group.add_argument('--model_name', type=str, default="unsloth/llama-3-8b", help="Model name to load")
176	    model_group.add_argument('--max_seq_length', type=int, default=2048, help="Maximum sequence length, default is 2048. We auto support RoPE Scaling internally!")
177	    model_group.add_argument('--dtype', type=str, default=None, help="Data type for model (None for auto detection)")
178	    model_group.add_argument('--load_in_4bit', action='store_true', help="Use 4bit quantization to reduce memory usage")
179	    model_group.add_argument('--dataset', type=str, default="yahma/alpaca-cleaned", help="Huggingface dataset to use for training")
180	
181	    lora_group = parser.add_argument_group("🧠 LoRA Options", "These options are used to configure the LoRA model.")
182	    lora_group.add_argument('--r', type=int, default=16, help="Rank for Lora model, default is 16.  (common values: 8, 16, 32, 64, 128)")
183	    lora_group.add_argument('--lora_alpha', type=int, default=16, help="LoRA alpha parameter, default is 16. (common values: 8, 16, 32, 64, 128)")
184	    lora_group.add_argument('--lora_dropout', type=float, default=0.0, help="LoRA dropout rate, default is 0.0 which is optimized.")
185	    lora_group.add_argument('--bias', type=str, default="none", help="Bias setting for LoRA")
186	    lora_group.add_argument('--use_gradient_checkpointing', type=str, default="unsloth", help="Use gradient checkpointing")
187	    lora_group.add_argument('--random_state', type=int, default=3407, help="Random state for reproducibility, default is 3407.")
188	    lora_group.add_argument('--use_rslora', action='store_true', help="Use rank stabilized LoRA")
189	    lora_group.add_argument('--loftq_config', type=str, default=None, help="Configuration for LoftQ")
190	
191	   
192	    training_group = parser.add_argument_group("🎓 Training Options")
193	    training_group.add_argument('--per_device_train_batch_size', type=int, default=2, help="Batch size per device during training, default is 2.")
194	    training_group.add_argument('--gradient_accumulation_steps', type=int, default=4, help="Number of gradient accumulation steps, default is 4.")
195	    training_group.add_argument('--warmup_steps', type=int, default=5, help="Number of warmup steps, default is 5.")
196	    training_group.add_argument('--max_steps', type=int, default=400, help="Maximum number of training steps.")
197	    training_group.add_argument('--learning_rate', type=float, default=2e-4, help="Learning rate, default is 2e-4.")
198	    training_group.add_argument('--optim', type=str, default="adamw_8bit", help="Optimizer type.")
199	    training_group.add_argument('--weight_decay', type=float, default=0.01, help="Weight decay, default is 0.01.")
200	    training_group.add_argument('--lr_scheduler_type', type=str, default="linear", help="Learning rate scheduler type, default is 'linear'.")
201	    training_group.add_argument('--seed', type=int, default=3407, help="Seed for reproducibility, default is 3407.")
202	    
203	
204	    # Report/Logging arguments
205	    report_group = parser.add_argument_group("📊 Report Options")
206	    report_group.add_argument('--report_to', type=str, default="tensorboard",
207	        choices=["azure_ml", "clearml", "codecarbon", "comet_ml", "dagshub", "dvclive", "flyte", "mlflow", "neptune", "tensorboard", "wandb", "all", "none"],
208	        help="The list of integrations to report the results and logs to. Supported platforms are: \n\t\t 'azure_ml', 'clearml', 'codecarbon', 'comet_ml', 'dagshub', 'dvclive', 'flyte', 'mlflow', 'neptune', 'tensorboard', and 'wandb'. Use 'all' to report to all integrations installed, 'none' for no integrations.")
209	    report_group.add_argument('--logging_steps', type=int, default=1, help="Logging steps, default is 1")
210	
211	    # Saving and pushing arguments
212	    save_group = parser.add_argument_group('💾 Save Model Options')
213	    save_group.add_argument('--output_dir', type=str, default="outputs", help="Output directory")
214	    save_group.add_argument('--save_model', action='store_true', help="Save the model after training")
215	    save_group.add_argument('--save_method', type=str, default="merged_16bit", choices=["merged_16bit", "merged_4bit", "lora"], help="Save method for the model, default is 'merged_16bit'")
216	    save_group.add_argument('--save_gguf', action='store_true', help="Convert the model to GGUF after training")
217	    save_group.add_argument('--save_path', type=str, default="model", help="Path to save the model")
218	    save_group.add_argument('--quantization', type=str, default="q8_0", nargs="+",
219	        help="Quantization method for saving the model. common values ('f16', 'q4_k_m', 'q8_0'), Check our wiki for all quantization methods https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/wiki#saving-to-gguf ")
220	
221	    push_group = parser.add_argument_group('🚀 Push Model Options')
222	    push_group.add_argument('--push_model', action='store_true', help="Push the model to Hugging Face hub after training")
223	    push_group.add_argument('--push_gguf', action='store_true', help="Push the model as GGUF to Hugging Face hub after training")
224	    push_group.add_argument('--hub_path', type=str, default="hf/model", help="Path on Hugging Face hub to push the model")
225	    push_group.add_argument('--hub_token', type=str, help="Token for pushing the model to Hugging Face hub")
226	
227	    args = parser.parse_args()
228	    run(args)

2142	# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
2143	def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
2144	    self,
2145	    tokenizer,
2146	    repo_id: str,
2147	    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
2148	    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
2149	    private: Optional[bool] = None,
2150	    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
2151	    create_pr: bool = False,
2152	    revision: str = None,
2153	    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
2154	    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
2155	    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
2156	):
2157	    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
2158	        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
2159	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
2160	            python_install.wait()
2161	            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
2162	            makefile = None
2163	        else:
2164	            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
2165	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
2166	            git_clone.wait()
2167	            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
2168	            python_install.wait()
2169	    else:
2170	        makefile = None
2171	
2172	    for _ in range(3):
2173	        gc.collect()
2174	
2175	    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
2176	    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)
2177	
2178	    model_type = self.config.model_type
2179	    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")
2180	
2181	    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
2182	    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")
2183	
2184	    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"
2185	
2186	    try:
2187	        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
2188	            for line in sp.stdout:
2189	                print(line, end="", flush=True)
2190	            for line in sp.stderr:
2191	                print(line, end="", flush=True)
2192	            sp.wait()
2193	            if sp.returncode != 0:
2194	                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)

1618	def create_ollama_modelfile(tokenizer, gguf_location):
1619	    """
1620	        Creates an Ollama Modelfile.
1621	        Use ollama.create(model = "new_ollama_model", modelfile = modelfile)
1622	    """
1623	    modelfile = getattr(tokenizer, "_ollama_modelfile", None)
1624	    if modelfile is None: return None
1625	
1626	    FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER = "⚫@✅#🦥__FILE_LOCATION__⚡@🦥#⛵"
1627	    EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER     = "⚫@✅#🦥__EOS_TOKEN__⚡@🦥#⛵"
1628	    LEFT_BRACKET_REPLACER  = "⚫@✅#🦥"
1629	    RIGHT_BRACKET_REPLACER = "⚡@🦥#⛵"
1630	
1631	    # Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1087
1632	    # We must convert all {'s and }'s but keep {__FILE_LOCATION__} intact
1633	    modelfile = modelfile\
1634	        .replace("{__FILE_LOCATION__}", FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER)\
1635	        .replace("{__EOS_TOKEN__}",     EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER)\
1636	        .replace("{", LEFT_BRACKET_REPLACER)\
1637	        .replace("}", RIGHT_BRACKET_REPLACER)
1638	
1639	    # Revert {__FILE_LOCATION__} back
1640	    modelfile = modelfile\
1641	        .replace(FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER, "{__FILE_LOCATION__}")\
1642	        .replace(EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER,     "{__EOS_TOKEN__}")
1643	
1644	    if "__EOS_TOKEN__" in modelfile:
1645	        modelfile = modelfile.format(
1646	            __FILE_LOCATION__  = gguf_location,
1647	            __EOS_TOKEN__      = tokenizer.eos_token,
1648	        )
1649	    else:
1650	        modelfile = modelfile.format(
1651	            __FILE_LOCATION__  = gguf_location,
1652	        )
1653	    pass
1654	
1655	    modelfile = modelfile\
1656	        .replace("⚫@✅#🦥", "{")\
1657	        .replace("⚡@🦥#⛵", "}")\
1658	        .rstrip()
1659	
1660	    return modelfile
1661	pass

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors

814	def try_execute(commands, force_complete = False):
815	    for command in commands:
816	        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell = True, stdout = subprocess.PIPE, stderr = subprocess.STDOUT, bufsize = 1) as sp:
817	            for line in sp.stdout:
818	                line = line.decode("utf-8", errors = "replace")
819	                if "undefined reference" in line:
820	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
821	                elif "deprecated" in line:
822	                    return "CMAKE"
823	                elif "Unknown argument" in line:
824	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
825	                elif "***" in line:
826	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
827	                print(line, flush = True, end = "")
828	            pass
829	            if force_complete and sp.returncode is not None and sp.returncode != 0:
830	                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, sp.args)
831	        pass
832	    pass
833	    return None

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

403	def fix_sentencepiece_gguf(saved_location):
404	    """
405	        Fixes sentencepiece tokenizers which did not extend the vocabulary with
406	        user defined tokens.
407	        Inspiration from https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/convert_hf_to_gguf.py
408	    """
409	    from copy import deepcopy
410	    from transformers.utils import sentencepiece_model_pb2
411	    import json
412	    from enum import IntEnum
413	    
414	    class SentencePieceTokenTypes(IntEnum):
415	        NORMAL = 1
416	        UNKNOWN = 2
417	        CONTROL = 3
418	        USER_DEFINED = 4
419	        UNUSED = 5
420	        BYTE = 6
421	    pass
422	
423	    # Load tokenizer.model
424	    tokenizer_file = sentencepiece_model_pb2.ModelProto()
425	    if not os.path.isfile(f"{saved_location}/tokenizer.model"): return
426	    tokenizer_file.ParseFromString(open(f"{saved_location}/tokenizer.model", "rb").read())
427	    sentence_piece_size = len(tokenizer_file.pieces)
428	
429	    # Load added_tokens_json
430	    if not os.path.isfile(f"{saved_location}/added_tokens.json"): return
431	    with open(f"{saved_location}/added_tokens.json", "r", encoding = "utf-8") as file:
432	        added_tokens_json = json.load(file)
433	    pass
434	    if len(added_tokens_json) == 0: return
435	
436	    added_tokens_json = dict(sorted(added_tokens_json.items(), key = lambda item: item[1]))
437	    new_size = sentence_piece_size + len(added_tokens_json)
438	
439	    # Confirm added_tokens_json is correct
440	    added_tokens_ids = np.array(list(added_tokens_json.values()))
441	    diff = np.diff(added_tokens_ids)
442	    if (diff.min() != 1 or diff.max() != 1): return
443	    if (added_tokens_ids.min() != sentence_piece_size): return
444	
445	    # Edit sentence piece tokens with added_tokens_json
446	    logger.warning(
447	        f"Unsloth: Extending {saved_location}/tokenizer.model with added_tokens.json.\n"\
448	        f"Originally tokenizer.model is of size ({sentence_piece_size}).\n"\
449	        f"But we need to extend to sentencepiece vocab size ({new_size})."
450	    )
451	    new_tokens = deepcopy(tokenizer_file.pieces[-len(added_tokens_ids):])
452	    for new_token, added_token in zip(new_tokens, added_tokens_json.keys()):
453	        new_token.piece = added_token.encode("utf-8")
454	        new_token.score = -1000.0
455	        new_token.type  = SentencePieceTokenTypes.USER_DEFINED
456	    pass
457	
458	    tokenizer_file.pieces.extend(new_tokens)
459	
460	    with open(f"{saved_location}/tokenizer.model", "wb") as file:
461	        file.write(tokenizer_file.SerializeToString())
462	    pass
463	
464	    # Add padding tokens
465	    # actual_vocab_size = model.config.vocab_size
466	    # padding = actual_vocab_size - len(tokenizer_file.pieces)
467	    return

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary document:

## Summary of GGUF Export Implementation in Unsloth

Based on my thorough analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's how GGUF export is implemented:

### Key Files

1. **Main implementation**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/save.py`
2. **Tokenizer utilities**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py`
3. **CLI example**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth-cli.py`

### Main GGUF Export Functions

#### 1. **User-Facing API** (attached to model object via `patch_saving_functions`)
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",  # Can be list like ["q4_k_m", "q8_0"]
    first_conversion: str = None,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    # ... other parameters
)

model.push_to_hub_gguf(
    repo_id: str,
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion: str = None,
    # ... other parameters
)
```

#### 2. **Core Implementation Functions**

**`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` [Line 1768]**
- Main entry point for saving to GGUF format
- Merges LoRA adapters into 16-bit weights (crucial requirement)
- Handles llama.cpp installation (non-blocking)
- Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
- Calls `save_to_gguf()` for actual GGUF conversion
- Generates Ollama Modelfile
- Handles tokenizer BOS token fixing

**`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` [Line 1951]**
- Similar to save but uploads to Hugging Face Hub
- Uses same underlying conversion pipeline

**`save_to_gguf()` [Line 952]**
- Core GGUF conversion orchestrator
- Handles llama.cpp executable detection/installation
- Manages multiple quantization methods
- Converts 4-bit/LoRA weights to first conversion format (f16, bf16, f32, or q8_0)
- Executes quantization via llama.cpp: `convert-hf-to-gguf.py` or `convert.py`
- Performs secondary quantization for q4_k_m, q5_k_m, etc. using `llama-quantize`
- Returns list of output file locations and whether full precision was saved

**`unsloth_save_model()` [Line 205]**
- Pre-processes model before GGUF conversion
- **Crucial for LoRA handling**: Calls `_merge_lora()` to merge LoRA weights into base weights
- Supports three save methods: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
- For GGUF, uses `save_method="merged_16bit"` (forced)
- Handles 4-bit quantization dequantization and merging
- Manages memory efficiently with disk spillover
- Extracts weights for all layers systematically

**`_merge_lora()` [Line 159]**
- **LoRA-specific function**: Extracts and merges LoRA weights
- Handles both 4-bit (bitsandbytes) and standard PEFT LoRA formats
- If model has LoRA adapters (detected via `get_lora_parameters_bias()`):
  - Dequantizes 4-bit weights if needed
  - Extracts LoRA matrices: A, B, and scaling factor s
  - Performs rank-1 update: `W += s * (A.T @ B.T)` via `addmm_`
  - Returns merged weights in appropriate dtype
- If no LoRA, returns raw layer weights

### GGUF Export Flow

```
1. User calls: model.save_pretrained_gguf(save_dir, tokenizer, quantization_method="q4_k_m")

2. unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf():
   - Fix tokenizer BOS token if needed
   - Install llama.cpp (non-blocking, parallel with model saving)
   - Call unsloth_save_model() with save_method="merged_16bit"
     
3. unsloth_save_model():
   - Iterate through all model layers
   - For each LoRA-adapted weight:
     - Call _merge_lora(layer, name)
       - Extract base weights (dequantize if 4-bit)
       - Extract LoRA matrices A, B and scale s
       - Merge: W = W + s * (A.T @ B.T)
     - Save merged weights to temporary location or memory
   - Save merged_16bit model to HuggingFace format
   
4. save_to_gguf(model_type, model_dtype="float16", ...):
   - Ensure llama.cpp tools available (quantize, convert scripts)
   - Determine optimal first_conversion format based on target quantization_methods
   - Fix sentencepiece tokenizer vocabulary if needed
   - Execute: python convert-hf-to-gguf.py <merged_16bit_dir> --outfile model.F16.gguf --outtype f16
   - Result: Full precision GGUF (e.g., unsloth.F16.GGUF)
   
5. For each quantization_method in ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", ...]:
   - Execute: ./llama-quantize unsloth.F16.GGUF unsloth.Q4_K_M.GGUF q4_k_m <num_cpus>
   - Result: Quantized GGUF files
   
6. Create Ollama Modelfile and upload if push_to_hub=True
```

### Key Design Details

**LoRA Weight Handling:**
- LoRA adapters are **always merged** before GGUF conversion (line 1826: `save_method="merged_16bit"`)
- LoRA is not preserved in the GGUF output - it's permanently merged
- Uses unsloth's fast LoRA merging via `get_lora_parameters_bias()` kernel function

**Supported Quantization Methods:**
```python
"not_quantized"   # -> f16/bf16
"fast_quantized"  # -> q8_0
"quantized"       # -> q4_k_m
"f32", "f16", "bf16", "q8_0"
"q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", "q2_k", "q3_k_l", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_s"
"q4_0", "q4_1", "q4_k_s", "q5_0", "q5_1", "q5_k_s", "q6_k"
```

**Integration with Huggingface:**
- Model is first saved as standard HuggingFace merged checkpoint
- Then converted to GGUF via llama.cpp's conversion scripts
- Metadata is patched (quantized_by, repo_url, tags)
- Support for both local saving and Hub pushing

**Memory Management:**
- Uses disk spillover for large models (temporary_location parameter)
- Handles GPU/CPU/Disk memory hierarchy intelligently
- Dequantizes 4-bit weights on-the-fly during merge
- Supports Kaggle/Colab environments with limited disk space

**External Dependencies:**
- **llama.cpp**: For HF-to-GGUF conversion and quantization
- **gguf** Python package: For GGUF metadata handling
- **protobuf**: For sentencepiece tokenizer modifications

### How Users Call It

From CLI (unsloth-cli.py):
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    args.save_path, 
    tokenizer, 
    quantization_method=args.quantization
)
```

From Python:
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    "output_model",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="q4_k_m",  # or list: ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "f16"]
    push_to_hub=True,
    token="hf_token"
)
```

The export process is wrapped as methods attached to the model object during initialization via `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2664).

2. **Tokenizer utilities**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py`
3. **CLI example**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth-cli.py`

### Main GGUF Export Functions

#### 1. **User-Facing API** (attached to model object via `patch_saving_functions`)
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",  # Can be list like ["q4_k_m", "q8_0"]
    first_conversion: str = None,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    # ... other parameters
)

model.push_to_hub_gguf(
    repo_id: str,
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion: str = None,
    # ... other parameters
)
```

#### 2. **Core Implementation Functions**

**`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` [Line 1768]**
- Main entry point for saving to GGUF format
- Merges LoRA adapters into 16-bit weights (crucial requirement)
- Handles llama.cpp installation (non-blocking)
- Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
- Calls `save_to_gguf()` for actual GGUF conversion
- Generates Ollama Modelfile
- Handles tokenizer BOS token fixing

**`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` [Line 1951]**
- Similar to save but uploads to Hugging Face Hub
- Uses same underlying conversion pipeline

**`save_to_gguf()` [Line 952]**
- Core GGUF conversion orchestrator
- Handles llama.cpp executable detection/installation
- Manages multiple quantization methods
- Converts 4-bit/LoRA weights to first conversion format (f16, bf16, f32, or q8_0)
- Executes quantization via llama.cpp: `convert-hf-to-gguf.py` or `convert.py`
- Performs secondary quantization for q4_k_m, q5_k_m, etc. using `llama-quantize`
- Returns list of output file locations and whether full precision was saved

**`unsloth_save_model()` [Line 205]**
- Pre-processes model before GGUF conversion
- **Crucial for LoRA handling**: Calls `_merge_lora()` to merge LoRA weights into base weights
- Supports three save methods: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
- For GGUF, uses `save_method="merged_16bit"` (forced)
- Handles 4-bit quantization dequantization and merging
- Manages memory efficiently with disk spillover
- Extracts weights for all layers systematically

**`_merge_lora()` [Line 159]**
- **LoRA-specific function**: Extracts and merges LoRA weights
- Handles both 4-bit (bitsandbytes) and standard PEFT LoRA formats
- If model has LoRA adapters (detected via `get_lora_parameters_bias()`):
  - Dequantizes 4-bit weights if needed
  - Extracts LoRA matrices: A, B, and scaling factor s
  - Performs rank-1 update: `W += s * (A.T @ B.T)` via `addmm_`
  - Returns merged weights in appropriate dtype
- If no LoRA, returns raw layer weights

### GGUF Export Flow

```
1. User calls: model.save_pretrained_gguf(save_dir, tokenizer, quantization_method="q4_k_m")

2. unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf():
   - Fix tokenizer BOS token if needed
   - Install llama.cpp (non-blocking, parallel with model saving)
   - Call unsloth_save_model() with save_method="merged_16bit"
     
3. unsloth_save_model():
   - Iterate through all model layers
   - For each LoRA-adapted weight:
     - Call _merge_lora(layer, name)
       - Extract base weights (dequantize if 4-bit)
       - Extract LoRA matrices A, B and scale s
       - Merge: W = W + s * (A.T @ B.T)
     - Save merged weights to temporary location or memory
   - Save merged_16bit model to HuggingFace format
   
4. save_to_gguf(model_type, model_dtype="float16", ...):
   - Ensure llama.cpp tools available (quantize, convert scripts)
   - Determine optimal first_conversion format based on target quantization_methods
   - Fix sentencepiece tokenizer vocabulary if needed
   - Execute: python convert-hf-to-gguf.py <merged_16bit_dir> --outfile model.F16.gguf --outtype f16
   - Result: Full precision GGUF (e.g., unsloth.F16.GGUF)
   
5. For each quantization_method in ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", ...]:
   - Execute: ./llama-quantize unsloth.F16.GGUF unsloth.Q4_K_M.GGUF q4_k_m <num_cpus>
   - Result: Quantized GGUF files
   
6. Create Ollama Modelfile and upload if push_to_hub=True
```

### Key Design Details

**LoRA Weight Handling:**
- LoRA adapters are **always merged** before GGUF conversion (line 1826: `save_method="merged_16bit"`)
- LoRA is not preserved in the GGUF output - it's permanently merged
- Uses unsloth's fast LoRA merging via `get_lora_parameters_bias()` kernel function

**Supported Quantization Methods:**
```python
"not_quantized"   # -> f16/bf16
"fast_quantized"  # -> q8_0
"quantized"       # -> q4_k_m
"f32", "f16", "bf16", "q8_0"
"q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", "q2_k", "q3_k_l", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_s"
"q4_0", "q4_1", "q4_k_s", "q5_0", "q5_1", "q5_k_s", "q6_k"
```

**Integration with Huggingface:**
- Model is first saved as standard HuggingFace merged checkpoint
- Then converted to GGUF via llama.cpp's conversion scripts
- Metadata is patched (quantized_by, repo_url, tags)
- Support for both local saving and Hub pushing

**Memory Management:**
- Uses disk spillover for large models (temporary_location parameter)
- Handles GPU/CPU/Disk memory hierarchy intelligently
- Dequantizes 4-bit weights on-the-fly during merge
- Supports Kaggle/Colab environments with limited disk space

**External Dependencies:**
- **llama.cpp**: For HF-to-GGUF conversion and quantization
- **gguf** Python package: For GGUF metadata handling
- **protobuf**: For sentencepiece tokenizer modifications

### How Users Call It

From CLI (unsloth-cli.py):
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    args.save_path, 
    tokenizer, 
    quantization_method=args.quantization
)
```

From Python:
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    "output_model",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="q4_k_m",  # or list: ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "f16"]
    push_to_hub=True,
    token="hf_token"
)
```

The export process is wrapped as methods attached to the model object during initialization via `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2664).

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary report:

## Unsloth Codebase Structure - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my very thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's the complete breakdown:

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Main directories:**
- `/unsloth/` - Main package root
  - `__init__.py` - Package initialization, imports key modules and patches
  - `save.py` - Model saving/exporting functionality (2,678 lines)
  - `chat_templates.py` - Chat template handling (129KB)
  - `tokenizer_utils.py` - Tokenizer utilities including GGUF fixing
  - `trainer.py` - Training argument classes and trainer patches
  - `import_fixes.py` - Compatibility fixes for imports
  
- `unsloth/models/` - Model implementations and loading
  - `loader.py` - Main model loading interface (FastLanguageModel, FastModel)
  - `llama.py` - Llama model optimizations (FastLlamaModel - base class, 131KB)
  - Individual model files: `mistral.py`, `qwen2.py`, `qwen3.py`, `gemma.py`, `gemma2.py`, `cohere.py`, `granite.py`, `falcon_h1.py`
  - `_utils.py` - Model utilities including PEFT patching (68KB)
  - `vision.py` - Vision model support
  - `rl.py`, `rl_replacements.py` - Reinforcement learning models
  - `mapper.py` - Model mapping utilities
  - `loader_utils.py` - Loader helper functions

- `unsloth/kernels/` - Optimized kernels
  - `fast_lora.py` - LoRA forward/backward implementations
  - Kernel optimizations: `geglu.py`, `layernorm.py`, `rms_layernorm.py`, `rope_embedding.py`, `flex_attention.py`, `cross_entropy_loss.py`
  - `moe/` - Mixture of Experts implementations

- `unsloth/registry/` - Model registry
  - `registry.py` - Base registry classes (ModelInfo, ModelMeta)
  - Model-specific registries: `_llama.py`, `_mistral.py`, `_qwen.py`, `_gemma.py`, `_deepseek.py`, `_phi.py`

- `unsloth/dataprep/` - Data preparation
  - `synthetic.py` - Synthetic data generation
  - `synthetic_configs.py` - Configuration for synthetic data

- `unsloth/utils/` - Utility functions

---

### 2. MODEL SAVING/EXPORTING FILES

**Primary save file:**
- `/unsloth/save.py` (2,678 lines) - Contains ALL saving/exporting functionality

**Key saving functions in save.py:**
- `unsloth_save_model()` - Main general-purpose save function
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` - Method to save merged models (16bit or 4bit)
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` - Method to push merged models to Hub
- `save_to_gguf()` - Core GGUF conversion (supports 20+ quantization methods)
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` - Method to save as GGUF with quantization
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` - Push GGUF models to Hub
- `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` - Custom directory LoRA saving
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` - GGML conversion
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` - GGML conversion + Hub push
- `unsloth_generic_save()` - Generic save for all model types
- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` - Generic merged save
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` - TorchAO quantization saving
- `patch_saving_functions()` - Patches model with custom save methods
- `create_huggingface_repo()` - HF repo creation
- `upload_to_huggingface()` - Upload to HF
- `push_to_ollama()` / `push_to_ollama_hub()` - Ollama integration

---

### 3. CLASSES HANDLING MODEL SAVING

**Main model classes with save capabilities:**

In `/unsloth/models/llama.py`:
- `FastLlamaModel` (line 1778) - Base model class for Llama-based architectures

In `/unsloth/models/loader.py`:
- `FastLanguageModel` (line 107) - Main API class, inherits FastLlamaModel
- `FastModel` (line 526) - Alternative model class
- `FastVisionModel` (line 995) - Vision models
- `FastTextModel` (line 998) - Text models

In `/unsloth/models/_utils.py`:
- Contains PEFT patching and LoRA integration functions

**Methods added to models by `patch_saving_functions()`:**
- `.save_pretrained()` - Standard HF method
- `.push_to_hub()` - Custom wrapper around HF push
- `.save_pretrained_merged()` - Merged weight saving
- `.push_to_hub_merged()` - Merged weight push
- `.save_pretrained_gguf()` - GGUF conversion and save
- `.push_to_hub_gguf()` - GGUF conversion and push

---

### 4. LORA-SPECIFIC LOGIC

**LoRA kernel implementations:**
- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - Main LoRA forward/backward pass
  - `LoRA_MLP` class - Fused LoRA MLP implementation
  - Functions: `get_lora_parameters()`, `get_lora_parameters_bias()`, `matmul_lora()`

**LoRA in model utilities:**
- `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` (lines 843-1626)
  - PEFT version imports and compatibility checks
  - `patch_fast_lora()` - Monkey patches PEFT's Linear4bit for fast LoRA
  - LoRA layer handling with `LoraLayer` from PEFT
  - LoftQ config validation

**PEFT integration:**
- Lines 18-19 of `/unsloth/save.py`:
  - `from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit`
  - `from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear`
- Lines 33 of `/unsloth/models/_utils.py`:
  - `from peft import LoraConfig`
- Lines 1413-1414 of `/unsloth/models/_utils.py`:
  - Direct patching of `peft.tuners.lora.bnb.Linear4bit.forward`

**LoRA saving:**
- `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` - Saves LoRA adapters as PEFT format
- `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` - Merges LoRA weights into base model

---

### 5. ONNX REFERENCES IN CODEBASE

**Result: NO ONNX references found**
- Grep search across entire codebase for "onnx" and "ONNX" returned zero matches
- Unsloth currently does NOT support ONNX export
- Focus is entirely on: HuggingFace format, GGUF, GGML, and Ollama

---

### 6. KEY DEPENDENCIES AND IMPORTS

**In save.py:**
```python
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from huggingface_hub import HfApi, get_token
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
```

**In models/_utils.py:**
```python
from peft import __version__ as peft_version
from peft.utils.integrations import dequantize_module_weight
from peft.tuners.lora.layer import LoraLayer
from peft import LoraConfig, LoftQConfig
```

**In models/loader.py:**
```python
from peft import PeftConfig, PeftModel
```

---

### 7. SAVE METHOD SUPPORT

The library supports these save methods (from line 286 of save.py):
1. **"lora"** - Saves LoRA modules only (fastest)
2. **"merged_16bit"** - Merges LoRA into float16 weights (for GGUF/llama.cpp)
3. **"merged_4bit"** - Merges LoRA into int4 weights (for DPO/HF inference)

---

### 8. GGUF QUANTIZATION METHODS SUPPORTED

The codebase supports 20+ quantization methods (from lines 79-104 of save.py):
- `not_quantized`, `fast_quantized`, `quantized`
- `f32`, `bf16`, `f16`, `q8_0`
- `q4_0`, `q4_1`, `q4_k_m`, `q4_k_s`, `q4_k`
- `q5_0`, `q5_1`, `q5_k_m`, `q5_k_s`, `q5_k`
- `q2_k`, `q3_k_l`, `q3_k_m`, `q3_k_s`, `q3_k_xs`
- `q6_k`
- `iq2_xxs`, `iq2_xs`, `iq3_xxs`

---

### 9. MAIN ENTRY POINTS

From `/unsloth/__init__.py`:
- Imports from models: `FastLanguageModel`, `FastModel`, etc.
- Imports from save: `unsloth_save_model`, `save_to_gguf`, `patch_saving_functions`, `create_huggingface_repo`
- Trainer: `UnslothTrainer`, `UnslothTrainingArguments`

---

### File Paths Summary

**Core Files (absolute paths):**
- `/unsloth/save.py` - 2,678 lines, ALL saving logic
- `/unsloth/models/loader.py` - FastLanguageModel, FastModel classes
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - FastLlamaModel base class (1,778+)
- `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` - PEFT patching, LoRA utilities
- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - LoRA MLP kernel
- `/unsloth/__init__.py` - Package initialization
- `/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` - GGUF tokenizer fixing

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report with all the findings.

## Comprehensive Report: Unsloth Adapter Saving and Merged Model Saving

Based on my thorough analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's how adapter saving and merged model saving work:

### Key File Locations

**Core Saving Implementation:**
- `/unsloth/save.py` (Main saving logic - 32,500+ lines)
- `/unsloth/models/loader_utils.py` (Model name/path utilities)
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (Model-specific implementations)
- `/unsloth/models/vision.py` (Vision model implementations)
- External dependency: `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils` (merge_and_overwrite_lora, prepare_saving)

**Test Files:**
- `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` (Main save tests)
- `/tests/saving/language_models/test_push_to_hub_merged.py` (Push to hub tests)
- `/tests/saving/language_models/test_merge_4bit_validation.py` (4-bit merge validation)

---

## 1. Save Method Definitions and Signatures

### Primary Entry Point: `unsloth_save_model()`
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:205`

```python
@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    # ... push_to_hub specific args
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
)
```

### User-Facing Methods (Attached via Monkey-Patching)

#### 1. `save_pretrained_merged()` - For Merged Models
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:1321`

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
)
```

#### 2. `push_to_hub_merged()` - For Pushing Merged Models to Hub
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:1364`

```python
def unsloth_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
)
```

#### 3. Generic Versions for Non-LLaMA Models
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2413, 2456`

- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` - Wrapper that calls `unsloth_generic_save()`
- `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` - Wrapper that calls `unsloth_generic_save()`

---

## 2. LoRA Weight Access Patterns

### Core LoRA Merging Function: `_merge_lora()`
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:159`

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        # ... dequantization logic ...
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        if A is not None:
            # Merge LoRA weights: W += (A^T @ B^T) * s
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
        # ...
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
```

### LoRA Parameter Extraction
**Imported from:** `/unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` → `get_lora_parameters_bias`

The function extracts:
- **W**: Base weight matrix
- **quant_state**: Quantization state (for 4-bit dequantization)
- **A**: LoRA adapter matrix A (low-rank)
- **B**: LoRA adapter matrix B (low-rank)
- **s**: LoRA scaling factor (alpha/rank)
- **bias**: Optional bias term

### Layer Pattern for Merged Models
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:567-602`

The merging iterates through layers using hardcoded patterns:

**Llama-specific weight projections:**
```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)

LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)
```

---

## 3. Adapter Metadata Extraction

### LoRA Configuration Storage
The PEFT model maintains adapter configuration in:
- `model.peft_config` - Dictionary of adapter configs (keyed by adapter name)
- Each config contains:
  - `r` (rank)
  - `lora_alpha` (scaling factor)
  - `target_modules` (list of modules to apply LoRA to)
  - `lora_dropout`
  - `bias`
  - Base model name/path

### State Dict Building
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:551-622`

```python
from collections import OrderedDict
state_dict = OrderedDict()

# For merged models (merged_16bit / merged_4bit):
# 1. Merge LoRA weights into base weights
# 2. Save full state dict with merged weights
# 3. Remove quantization_config from model.config for 16-bit

# For LoRA-only models:
# Uses model.save_pretrained() which delegates to PEFT's save_pretrained()
```

### Tags and Metadata
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:305-311`

```python
if tags is not None:
    assert(isinstance(tags, (list, tuple)))
    tags = list(tags) + ["unsloth",]
else:
    tags = ["unsloth",]
pass
save_pretrained_settings["tags"] = tags
```

---

## 4. How Save Methods Are Exposed to Users (Monkey-Patching Pattern)

### The `patch_saving_functions()` Function
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2569`

This is the key function that attaches all save methods to model instances:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List
    
    # Step 1: Store original push_to_hub
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    
    # Step 2: Dynamically create unsloth_push_to_hub() wrapper
    # (captures original signature and docs via inspect.signature())
    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    # ... exec() to create function dynamically ...
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
    
    # Step 3: Attach methods via types.MethodType
    original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
    original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
    
    # Step 4: Add new Unsloth-specific methods for non-vision models
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
    else:
        # Vision models get fewer options
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        # ...
```

### Method Attachment Mechanism: `types.MethodType()`

The pattern is:
```python
types.MethodType(function, instance)
```

This binds a function to an instance, making it a bound method. All save/push methods are attached this way.

### When Patching Occurs
**Called in:**
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - After creating PEFT model
- `/unsloth/models/vision.py` - For vision models
- `/unsloth/chat_templates.py` - For tokenizers

---

## 5. Push to Hub Implementation

### Enhanced `push_to_hub()` Wrapper
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2586-2639`

The wrapper is created dynamically via `exec()` to match the original model's signature:

```python
push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    
    # Add "unsloth" tag
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    
    # Enhance commit messages
    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message
    
    # Upload to huggingface
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    
    # Call original push_to_hub
    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    '''
```

### Merged Model Push: `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()`
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2456`

This method:
1. Extracts arguments from locals()
2. Sets `save_directory = repo_id` and `push_to_hub = True`
3. Calls `unsloth_generic_save()` which delegates to `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from `unsloth_zoo`

---

## 6. Save Method Workflow

### Three Save Modes

1. **`save_method="lora"`** - LoRA adapters only
   - Fast, small file size
   - Uses PEFT's native save_pretrained()
   - Saves adapter_config.json and adapter weight files

2. **`save_method="merged_16bit"`** - LoRA merged into float16
   - Recommended for GGUF/llama.cpp
   - Merges LoRA into base weights
   - Converts 4-bit weights to float16
   - Removes quantization_config

3. **`save_method="merged_4bit"`** - LoRA merged into int4
   - Uses BitsAndBytes int4 format
   - Requires `merge_and_unload()` call
   - Preserves quantization_config

### Merged Model Saving Flow (merged_16bit)

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:483-748`

```
1. Check save_method validity
2. For 4-bit models: call model.merge_and_unload()
3. Build OrderedDict state_dict
4. Iterate through model layers:
   - For each LLAMA_WEIGHTS projection:
     - Extract LoRA (A, B, s from get_lora_parameters_bias)
     - Dequantize if 4-bit
     - Merge: W += (A^T @ B^T) * alpha/rank
     - Store merged weight in state_dict
   - For each LLAMA_LAYERNORMS:
     - Copy layer norm weights directly
5. Memory management:
   - Check VRAM availability
   - Spill to disk if needed using torch.save()
6. Save state_dict to model.save_pretrained(..., state_dict=state_dict)
7. If push_to_hub:
   - Call upload_to_huggingface() for metadata
   - Upload tokenizer
   - Create model card with unsloth tags
```

---

## 7. External Dependencies

### `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils`

**Location:** External package (not in this repo)

Key imports from this module:
- `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` - Generic model merging for non-LLaMA architectures
- `prepare_saving()` - Prepares model state for saving

Called in `unsloth_generic_save()`:
```python
merge_and_overwrite_lora(
    get_model_name,
    model                = model,
    tokenizer            = tokenizer,
    save_directory       = save_directory,
    push_to_hub          = push_to_hub,
    private              = private,
    token                = token,
    save_method          = save_method,
    output_dtype         = None,
    low_disk_space_usage = True,
    use_temp_file        = False,
)
```

---

## 8. Complete Save Method Exposure Pattern

### Pattern Summary

1. **Monkey-patching via `patch_saving_functions()`**
   - Called at model creation time (in `get_peft_model()`)
   - Attaches methods to model instance using `types.MethodType()`
   
2. **Method Binding**
   ```python
   model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
       unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, 
       model
   )
   ```

3. **User-Facing API**
   ```python
   model.save_pretrained_merged(
       save_directory="./my_model",
       tokenizer=tokenizer,
       save_method="merged_16bit"
   )
   
   model.push_to_hub_merged(
       repo_id="username/model-name",
       tokenizer=tokenizer,
       token=hf_token
   )
   ```

4. **Wrapper Functions**
   - All methods are wrappers around core `unsloth_save_model()` or `unsloth_generic_save()`
   - Extract arguments from `locals()` and pass as dict
   - Handle tokenizer separately with own save settings

---

## Summary of Key Files and Patterns

| Component | File Path | Key Elements |
|-----------|-----------|--------------|
| Core saving logic | `/unsloth/save.py` | `unsloth_save_model()`, `_merge_lora()`, state_dict building |
| LoRA weight access | `/unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` | `get_lora_parameters_bias()` extracts A, B, s matrices |
| Method exposure | `/unsloth/save.py:2569` | `patch_saving_functions()` uses `types.MethodType()` to attach methods |
| User entry points | `/unsloth/models/llama.py`, `/unsloth/models/vision.py` | Calls `patch_saving_functions()` after `get_peft_model()` |
| Save method implementations | `/unsloth/save.py:1321, 1364, 2413, 2456` | `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()`, `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()`, generic variants |
| External merging | `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils` | `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` for non-LLaMA models |

Based on my thorough analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's how adapter saving and merged model saving work:

## 1. Save Method Definitions and Signatures

### Primary Entry Point: `unsloth_save_model()`
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:205`

```python
@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,

```

### User-Facing Methods (Attached via Monkey-Patching)

#### 1. `save_pretrained_merged()` - For Merged Models
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:1321`

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,

```

#### 2. `push_to_hub_merged()` - For Pushing Merged Models to Hub
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:1364`

```python
def unsloth_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
```

#### 3. Generic Versions for Non-LLaMA Models
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2413, 2456`

- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` - Wrapper that calls `unsloth_generic_save()`
- `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` - Wrapper that calls `unsloth_generic_save()`

---

## 2. LoRA Weight Access Patterns

### Core LoRA Merging Function: `_merge_lora()`
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:159`

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        # ... dequantization logic ...
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        if A is not None:
            # Merge LoRA weights: W += (A^T @ B^T) * s
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
        # ...
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
```

### LoRA Parameter Extraction
**Imported from:** `/unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` → `get_lora_parameters_bias`

The function extracts:
- **W**: Base weight matrix
- **quant_state**: Quantization state (for 4-bit dequantization)
- **A**: LoRA adapter matrix A (low-rank)
- **B**: LoRA adapter matrix B (low-rank)
- **s**: LoRA scaling factor (alpha/rank)
- **bias**: Optional bias term

### Layer Pattern for Merged Models
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:567-602`

The merging iterates through layers using hardcoded patterns:

**Llama-specific weight projections:**
```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)

LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)
```

---

## 3. Adapter Metadata Extraction

### LoRA Configuration Storage
The PEFT model maintains adapter configuration in:
- `model.peft_config` - Dictionary of adapter configs (keyed by adapter name)
- Each config contains:
  - `r` (rank)
  - `lora_alpha` (scaling factor)
  - `target_modules` (list of modules to apply LoRA to)
  - `lora_dropout`
  - `bias`
  - Base model name/path

### State Dict Building
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:551-622`

```python
from collections import OrderedDict
state_dict = OrderedDict()

# For merged models (merged_16bit / merged_4bit):
# 1. Merge LoRA weights into base weights
# 2. Save full state dict with merged weights
# 3. Remove quantization_config from model.config for 16-bit

# For LoRA-only models:
# Uses model.save_pretrained() which delegates to PEFT's save_pretrained()
```

### Tags and Metadata
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:305-311`

```python
if tags is not None:
    assert(isinstance(tags, (list, tuple)))
    tags = list(tags) + ["unsloth",]
else:
    tags = ["unsloth",]
pass
save_pretrained_settings["tags"] = tags
```

---

## 4. How Save Methods Are Exposed to Users (Monkey-Patching Pattern)

### The `patch_saving_functions()` Function
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2569`

This is the key function that attaches all save methods to model instances:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List
    
    # Step 1: Store original push_to_hub
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    
    # Step 2: Dynamically create unsloth_push_to_hub() wrapper
    # (captures original signature and docs via inspect.signature())
    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    # ... exec() to create function dynamically ...
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
    
    # Step 3: Attach methods via types.MethodType
    original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
    original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
    
    # Step 4: Add new Unsloth-specific methods for non-vision models
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
    else:
        # Vision models get fewer options
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        # ...
```

### Method Attachment Mechanism: `types.MethodType()`

The pattern is:
```python
types.MethodType(function, instance)
```

This binds a function to an instance, making it a bound method. All save/push methods are attached this way.

### When Patching Occurs
**Called in:**
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - After creating PEFT model
- `/unsloth/models/vision.py` - For vision models
- `/unsloth/chat_templates.py` - For tokenizers

---

## 5. Push to Hub Implementation

### Enhanced `push_to_hub()` Wrapper
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2586-2639`

The wrapper is created dynamically via `exec()` to match the original model's signature:

```python
push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    
    # Add "unsloth" tag
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    
    # Enhance commit messages
    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message
    
    # Upload to huggingface
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    
    # Call original push_to_hub
    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    '''
```

### Merged Model Push: `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()`
**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2456`

This method:
1. Extracts arguments from locals()
2. Sets `save_directory = repo_id` and `push_to_hub = True`
3. Calls `unsloth_generic_save()` which delegates to `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from `unsloth_zoo`

---

## 6. Save Method Workflow

### Three Save Modes

1. **`save_method="lora"`** - LoRA adapters only
   - Fast, small file size
   - Uses PEFT's native save_pretrained()
   - Saves adapter_config.json and adapter weight files

2. **`save_method="merged_16bit"`** - LoRA merged into float16
   - Recommended for GGUF/llama.cpp
   - Merges LoRA into base weights
   - Converts 4-bit weights to float16
   - Removes quantization_config

3. **`save_method="merged_4bit"`** - LoRA merged into int4
   - Uses BitsAndBytes int4 format
   - Requires `merge_and_unload()` call
   - Preserves quantization_config

### Merged Model Saving Flow (merged_16bit)

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:483-748`

```
1. Check save_method validity
2. For 4-bit models: call model.merge_and_unload()
3. Build OrderedDict state_dict
4. Iterate through model layers:
   - For each LLAMA_WEIGHTS projection:
     - Extract LoRA (A, B, s from get_lora_parameters_bias)
     - Dequantize if 4-bit
     - Merge: W += (A^T @ B^T) * alpha/rank
     - Store merged weight in state_dict
   - For each LLAMA_LAYERNORMS:
     - Copy layer norm weights directly
5. Memory management:
   - Check VRAM availability
   - Spill to disk if needed using torch.save()
6. Save state_dict to model.save_pretrained(..., state_dict=state_dict)
7. If push_to_hub:
   - Call upload_to_huggingface() for metadata
   - Upload tokenizer
   - Create model card with unsloth tags
```

---

## 7. External Dependencies

### `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils`

**Location:** External package (not in this repo)

Key imports from this module:
- `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` - Generic model merging for non-LLaMA architectures
- `prepare_saving()` - Prepares model state for saving

Called in `unsloth_generic_save()`:
```python
merge_and_overwrite_lora(
    get_model_name,
    model                = model,
    tokenizer            = tokenizer,
    save_directory       = save_directory,
    push_to_hub          = push_to_hub,
    private              = private,
    token                = token,
    save_method          = save_method,
    output_dtype         = None,
    low_disk_space_usage = True,
    use_temp_file        = False,
)
```

---

## 8. Complete Save Method Exposure Pattern

### Pattern Summary

1. **Monkey-patching via `patch_saving_functions()`**
   - Called at model creation time (in `get_peft_model()`)
   - Attaches methods to model instance using `types.MethodType()`
   
2. **Method Binding**
   ```python
   model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(
       unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, 
       model
   )
   ```

3. **User-Facing API**
   ```python
   model.save_pretrained_merged(
       save_directory="./my_model",
       tokenizer=tokenizer,
       save_method="merged_16bit"
   )
   
   model.push_to_hub_merged(
       repo_id="username/model-name",
       tokenizer=tokenizer,
       token=hf_token
   )
   ```

4. **Wrapper Functions**
   - All methods are wrappers around core `unsloth_save_model()` or `unsloth_generic_save()`
   - Extract arguments from `locals()` and pass as dict
   - Handle tokenizer separately with own save settings

---

## Summary of Key Files and Patterns

| Component | File Path | Key Elements |
|-----------|-----------|--------------|
| Core saving logic | `/unsloth/save.py` | `unsloth_save_model()`, `_merge_lora()`, state_dict building |
| LoRA weight access | `/unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` | `get_lora_parameters_bias()` extracts A, B, s matrices |
| Method exposure | `/unsloth/save.py:2569` | `patch_saving_functions()` uses `types.MethodType()` to attach methods |
| User entry points | `/unsloth/models/llama.py`, `/unsloth/models/vision.py` | Calls `patch_saving_functions()` after `get_peft_model()` |
| Save method implementations | `/unsloth/save.py:1321, 1364, 2413, 2456` | `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()`, `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()`, generic variants |
| External merging | `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils` | `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` for non-LLaMA models |

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return

gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass
