1769	    self,
1770	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
1771	    tokenizer            = None,
1772	    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
1773	    first_conversion     : str = None,
1774	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
1775	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
1776	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
1777	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
1778	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
1779	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
1780	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
1781	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
1782	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
1783	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
1784	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
1785	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
1786	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
1787	):
1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            python_install.wait()
        pass
    else:
        try:
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        except:
            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp
            if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
                # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?

def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

):

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.

@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_generic_save(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike] = "unsloth_finetuned_merge",
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"

    merge_and_overwrite_lora(
        get_model_name,
        model                = model,
        tokenizer            = tokenizer,

input.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for adding ONNX export support for LoRA adapters in the unsloth codebase.

## Codebase Context

The repo root is at: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/`

### Key Files:
- `unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines) — ALL saving/exporting logic lives here
- `unsloth/__init__.py` — Does `from .save import *` at line 263; `__all__` in save.py controls exports
- `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` — Contains `get_lora_parameters_bias()` at line 235
- `unsloth/models/llama.py` — Calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` at lines 2204 and 2902
- `unsloth/models/vision.py` — Calls `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=True)` at lines 702, 918, 964

### How Existing Exports Work:

**Pattern for registering methods (in `patch_saving_functions()` at line 2569 of save.py):**
Methods are monkey-patched onto model instances using `types.MethodType()`:
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf     = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
# etc.
```
This happens at lines 2658-2676 inside an `if not vision:` block (with `if hasattr(model, "config"):`).

**`__all__` in save.py (line 48-54):**
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

**LoRA weight extraction (from `get_lora_parameters_bias` in kernels/utils.py:235):**
```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), proj.lora_A[adapter].weight, proj.lora_B[adapter].weight, proj.scaling[adapter], base_layer.bias)
```
Returns: (W, quant_state, A_weight, B_weight, scaling, bias)
- `scaling` = alpha/rank
- A shape: (rank, in_features), B shape: (out_features, rank)

**How `_merge_lora()` works (save.py:159):**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        # dequantize if 4-bit...
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        if A is not None:
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
```

**GGUF export signature (save.py:1768):**
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion: str = None,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    ...
):
```

**Imports at top of save.py:**
```python
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from typing import Optional, Callable, Union, List
import torch, os, shutil, gc
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
```

**PEFT config access:**
- `model.peft_config` is a dict of adapter configs
- Each config has: `r` (rank), `lora_alpha`, `target_modules`, `lora_dropout`, `bias`
- The active adapter name is typically "default"

### Requirements:
1. Add an `export_to_onnx` method (as a function in save.py + register in `patch_saving_functions`)
2. The method should:
   - Extract LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) — NOT merge them
   - Create an ONNX-compatible representation of the LoRA adapter
   - Save to a specified output directory
   - Include metadata (rank, alpha, target modules)
3. Parameters: `output_dir`, `opset_version` (default 17)
4. Add docstrings and type hints
5. Register/expose alongside existing save methods

### Design Considerations:
- This exports just the LoRA adapter as ONNX (not the full model) — so we iterate LoRA layers, extract A and B weight tensors, and create ONNX graph nodes
- The ONNX graph should represent the LoRA computation: output = x @ A^T @ B^T * scaling
- We should save metadata (rank, alpha, target_modules) in ONNX model metadata_props
- Use `onnx` Python package (optional import, raise helpful error if not installed)
- Follow the existing pattern: define function, register via `types.MethodType` in `patch_saving_functions`

Please create a detailed step-by-step implementation plan covering:
1. Exactly which files to modify and what to add
2. The function signature and implementation approach
3. How to extract LoRA weights without merging
4. How to build the ONNX representation
5. How to register the new method
6. What to add to __all__

content.text:
I now have a complete understanding of the codebase. Let me produce the detailed implementation plan.

---

## Detailed Implementation Plan: ONNX Export for LoRA Adapters

### Overview

This plan adds an `export_to_onnx` method to unsloth models that exports the LoRA adapter (A and B weight matrices, plus scaling metadata) as an ONNX model -- without merging the weights into the base model. The ONNX graph represents the LoRA computation: `output = x @ A^T @ B^T * scaling` for each target module.

Only one file requires substantive changes: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/save.py`. No other files need modification.

---

### Step 1: Add the `onnx` optional import guard (save.py, near top of file, around line 46)

Insert a lazy-import helper pattern near the existing imports. This follows the same pattern the codebase uses elsewhere (e.g., the `try/except` for `get_token` at lines 36-44). The actual import will be done inside the function body, but we define a helper to give a clear error message:

```python
def _check_onnx_installed():
    try:
        import onnx
        return onnx
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
            "Install it via: pip install onnx"
        )
```

Place this helper function after the `__all__` declaration (after line 54) and before the `LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS` constant at line 57.

---

### Step 2: Add `"unsloth_export_to_onnx"` to `__all__` (save.py, line 48-54)

Change the `__all__` list from:

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

to:

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "unsloth_export_to_onnx",
]
```

No changes needed in `unsloth/__init__.py` since it already does `from .save import *` at line 263 and will automatically pick up anything in `__all__`.

---

### Step 3: Define the `unsloth_export_to_onnx` function (save.py, insert before `patch_saving_functions` at line 2569)

A good insertion point is around line 2562, right after the `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` function ends and before `not_implemented_save` at line 2564. This keeps it grouped with the other export/save functions.

#### Function signature:

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def unsloth_export_to_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir      : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version   : int = 17,
) -> str:
```

**Parameters:**
- `self` -- the model instance (will be bound via `types.MethodType`)
- `output_dir` -- directory path where the ONNX file and metadata JSON will be saved
- `opset_version` -- ONNX opset version (default 17, which supports all needed ops: MatMul, Mul, Add)

**Returns:** the path to the saved `.onnx` file as a string.

#### Docstring:

```python
"""
Export LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format without merging into the base model.

Creates an ONNX model representing the LoRA computation for each target module:
    output = x @ A^T @ B^T * scaling

The adapter's rank, alpha, scaling factor, and target module names are stored
as ONNX model metadata_props.

Args:
    output_dir: Directory where the ONNX file will be saved. Created if it does not exist.
    opset_version: ONNX opset version to use. Default is 17.

Returns:
    Path to the saved ONNX model file.

Raises:
    ImportError: If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.
    ValueError: If no LoRA adapters are found on the model.
"""
```

#### Implementation logic (pseudocode with key details):

**3a. Check onnx is installed:**
```python
onnx = _check_onnx_installed()
from onnx import helper, TensorProto, numpy_helper
import numpy as np
```

**3b. Extract the active adapter name and peft config:**
```python
# Get active adapter name - follows the pattern from kernels/utils.py lines 245-246
adapter_name = getattr(self, "active_adapters", None)
if adapter_name is None:
    adapter_name = getattr(self, "active_adapter", ("default",))
adapter_name = adapter_name[0] if isinstance(adapter_name, (list, tuple)) else adapter_name

# Get peft config for metadata
peft_config = self.peft_config[adapter_name]
rank = peft_config.r
lora_alpha = peft_config.lora_alpha
target_modules = list(peft_config.target_modules)
```

**3c. Iterate all named modules, find LoRA layers, extract A and B weights:**

Use `model.named_modules()` (as done throughout the codebase, e.g., in `unsloth/models/llama.py:2249`, `unsloth/models/granite.py:517`) and check for `isinstance(module, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear))` (same guard as `_merge_lora` at line 162).

For each LoRA layer, call `get_lora_parameters_bias(module)` to extract `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)`. If `A is None`, the layer has no active LoRA adapter, so skip it.

```python
lora_layers = {}  # Maps module_name -> (A_weight, B_weight, scaling)

for name, module in self.named_modules():
    if not isinstance(module, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        continue
    
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(module)
    if A is None:
        continue  # No active LoRA adapter on this layer
    
    # A shape: (rank, in_features)
    # B shape: (out_features, rank)
    # s: scalar scaling = alpha / rank
    lora_layers[name] = (
        A.detach().cpu().float().numpy(),
        B.detach().cpu().float().numpy(),
        float(s),
    )

if len(lora_layers) == 0:
    raise ValueError("Unsloth: No LoRA adapters found on the model. Cannot export to ONNX.")
```

**3d. Build the ONNX graph:**

The ONNX graph represents a LoRA adapter module that takes input activations and returns the LoRA delta for each target layer. For each LoRA layer, the subgraph computes: `delta = (x @ A^T) @ B^T * scaling`.

The structure: one ONNX graph with a separate set of nodes per LoRA layer. Each layer gets:
- Two initializer tensors: `{name}.lora_A` and `{name}.lora_B`
- A scalar constant: `{name}.scaling`
- One input: `{name}.input` (shape: [batch, seq_len, in_features])
- One output: `{name}.output` (shape: [batch, seq_len, out_features])
- Three operations: MatMul(input, A^T) -> MatMul(result, B^T) -> Mul(result, scaling)

```python
initializers = []
nodes = []
inputs = []
outputs = []

for layer_name, (A_np, B_np, scaling) in lora_layers.items():
    # Sanitize the layer name for ONNX node naming (replace '.' with '/')
    safe_name = layer_name.replace(".", "/")
    
    rank, in_features = A_np.shape
    out_features, _ = B_np.shape
    
    # Create initializer tensors for A^T and B^T (pre-transposed for MatMul)
    A_T = A_np.T  # Shape: (in_features, rank)
    B_T = B_np.T  # Shape: (rank, out_features)
    
    init_A = numpy_helper.from_array(A_T.astype(np.float32), name=f"{safe_name}/lora_A_T")
    init_B = numpy_helper.from_array(B_T.astype(np.float32), name=f"{safe_name}/lora_B_T")
    init_s = numpy_helper.from_array(
        np.array(scaling, dtype=np.float32), name=f"{safe_name}/scaling"
    )
    initializers.extend([init_A, init_B, init_s])
    
    # Input for this layer
    inp = helper.make_tensor_value_info(
        f"{safe_name}/input", TensorProto.FLOAT, [None, None, in_features]
    )
    inputs.append(inp)
    
    # Nodes: x @ A^T -> intermediate @ B^T -> result * scaling
    node_matmul_A = helper.make_node(
        "MatMul",
        inputs=[f"{safe_name}/input", f"{safe_name}/lora_A_T"],
        outputs=[f"{safe_name}/after_A"],
        name=f"{safe_name}/matmul_A",
    )
    node_matmul_B = helper.make_node(
        "MatMul",
        inputs=[f"{safe_name}/after_A", f"{safe_name}/lora_B_T"],
        outputs=[f"{safe_name}/after_B"],
        name=f"{safe_name}/matmul_B",
    )
    node_scale = helper.make_node(
        "Mul",
        inputs=[f"{safe_name}/after_B", f"{safe_name}/scaling"],
        outputs=[f"{safe_name}/output"],
        name=f"{safe_name}/scale",
    )
    nodes.extend([node_matmul_A, node_matmul_B, node_scale])
    
    # Output for this layer
    out = helper.make_tensor_value_info(
        f"{safe_name}/output", TensorProto.FLOAT, [None, None, out_features]
    )
    outputs.append(out)
```

**3e. Assemble the ONNX model with metadata:**

```python
graph = helper.make_graph(
    nodes,
    "lora_adapter",
    inputs,
    outputs,
    initializer=initializers,
)

model_proto = helper.make_model(graph, opset_imports=[helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)])
model_proto.ir_version = 8  # Compatible with opset 17

# Add metadata properties
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="lora_rank", value=str(rank))
)
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="lora_alpha", value=str(lora_alpha))
)
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="target_modules", value=",".join(target_modules))
)
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="adapter_name", value=adapter_name)
)
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="num_lora_layers", value=str(len(lora_layers)))
)
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="exported_by", value="unsloth")
)

# Validate the model
onnx.checker.check_model(model_proto)
```

**3f. Save the ONNX model to disk:**

```python
os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_adapter.onnx")
onnx.save(model_proto, output_path)

print(f"Unsloth: LoRA adapter exported to ONNX at {output_path}")
print(f"  Adapter: {adapter_name}, Rank: {rank}, Alpha: {lora_alpha}")
print(f"  Target modules: {target_modules}")
print(f"  Number of LoRA layers exported: {len(lora_layers)}")

return output_path
```

---

### Step 4: Register the method in `patch_saving_functions` (save.py, inside `patch_saving_functions` starting at line 2569)

Add the registration in two places:

**4a. In the `if not vision:` block (line 2658), inside the `if hasattr(model, "config"):` guard (line 2659), add after line 2667:**

```python
model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)
```

This goes right after the `model.save_pretrained_ggml` assignment at line 2667.

**4b. In the `else:` block for vision models (line 2669), add after line 2675:**

```python
model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)
```

The ONNX export operates generically on LoRA layers via `named_modules()` and `get_lora_parameters_bias()`, so it works for both text and vision models without needing any model-architecture-specific code like `LLAMA_WEIGHTS`. This is a key advantage over the GGUF export path which is architecture-specific.

---

### Step 5: Summary of all changes by file

**File: `unsloth/save.py`**

| Location | Change |
|---|---|
| Lines 48-54 (`__all__`) | Add `"unsloth_export_to_onnx"` to the list |
| After line 54, before line 57 | Add `_check_onnx_installed()` helper function |
| After line 2561 (after `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` ends) | Add the `unsloth_export_to_onnx` function (~100-120 lines) |
| Line 2667 (inside `if not vision: if hasattr(model, "config"):`) | Add `model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)` |
| Line 2675 (inside `else:` vision block) | Add `model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)` |

**No other files need changes.** The `unsloth/__init__.py` already does `from .save import *` which will pick up the new export from the updated `__all__`.

---

### Design Decisions and Rationale

1. **Why store A^T and B^T (pre-transposed) as initializers instead of A and B with Transpose nodes?** Fewer ONNX nodes means simpler graph and faster inference. The ONNX MatMul op computes `input @ weight`, and since `get_lora_parameters_bias` returns A with shape `(rank, in_features)`, we need `x @ A^T` which is `x @ A.T`. Pre-transposing avoids runtime Transpose operations.

2. **Why one graph with per-layer I/O rather than separate ONNX models per layer?** A single file is easier to distribute and load. The per-layer inputs/outputs allow runtime frameworks to feed activations from the appropriate base model layer. Naming them with the layer path (e.g., `model/layers/0/self_attn/q_proj/input`) provides a clear mapping back to the base model.

3. **Why use `named_modules()` iteration instead of `LLAMA_WEIGHTS`?** The `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` constant is architecture-specific (llama only). Using `named_modules()` with `isinstance` checks works for all architectures (llama, gemma, granite, vision models). This is the same pattern used in `_merge_lora()` -- the `isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear))` check at line 162.

4. **Why use `get_lora_parameters_bias` instead of accessing `lora_A`/`lora_B` directly?** This function already handles edge cases: finding the active adapter, dealing with disabled/merged adapters, and extracting the correct scaling factor. Reusing it avoids duplicating that logic.

5. **Why `.` to `/` name sanitization?** ONNX node names with dots can confuse some ONNX tools. Using `/` is the conventional separator in ONNX graphs and avoids issues with downstream parsers.

6. **Why not include `torch.onnx.export`?** `torch.onnx.export` traces an entire `nn.Module` forward pass. Here we are only exporting the LoRA delta computation (not the base model), so building the ONNX graph manually with the `onnx` helper API is more direct and avoids needing the base model weights or a forward pass trace.

---

### Potential Challenges

1. **Large number of LoRA layers**: A 70B model with LoRA on all attention+MLP projections across 80 layers = 560 LoRA layers. The ONNX file could be large. This is acceptable since it is fundamentally just the adapter weights, but we should print the file size after saving.

2. **Mixed-rank LoRA**: Some configurations use different ranks for different modules. The current approach handles this naturally since we extract per-layer, but the metadata `lora_rank` would only reflect the config's single `r` value. Consider adding per-layer rank info as additional metadata entries or a JSON sidecar if needed. For V1, the single config rank is sufficient since unsloth enforces a uniform rank per adapter.

3. **Multiple active adapters**: The current implementation only exports the first active adapter (`adapter_name[0]`). This matches how the rest of unsloth handles adapters. Supporting multi-adapter export could be a future enhancement.

4. **`onnx` package not installed**: The lazy import with a clear error message in `_check_onnx_installed()` ensures a good user experience. The `onnx` package should NOT be added as a hard dependency in setup.py/requirements -- it is optional.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/save.py` -- The primary file. All new code goes here: the `_check_onnx_installed` helper, the `unsloth_export_to_onnx` function, the `__all__` update, and the `patch_saving_functions` registration.
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` -- Contains `get_lora_parameters_bias()` at line 235, which is the key dependency for extracting LoRA weights. Not modified, but must be understood.
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/__init__.py` -- Does `from .save import *` at line 263. Not modified, but confirms automatic re-export via `__all__`.
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/models/llama.py` -- Calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` at lines 2204 and 2902. Not modified, but confirms the registration mechanism works for text models.
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/models/vision.py` -- Calls `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=True)` at lines 702, 918, 964. Not modified, but confirms vision model registration path.

Design an implementation plan for adding ONNX export support for LoRA adapters in the unsloth codebase.

## Codebase Context

The repo root is at: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/`

### Key Files:
- `unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines) — ALL saving/exporting logic lives here
- `unsloth/__init__.py` — Does `from .save import *` at line 263; `__all__` in save.py controls exports
- `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` — Contains `get_lora_parameters_bias()` at line 235
- `unsloth/models/llama.py` — Calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` at lines 2204 and 2902
- `unsloth/models/vision.py` — Calls `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=True)` at lines 702, 918, 964

### How Existing Exports Work:

**Pattern for registering methods (in `patch_saving_functions()` at line 2569 of save.py):**
Methods are monkey-patched onto model instances using `types.MethodType()`:
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf     = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
# etc.
```
This happens at lines 2658-2676 inside an `if not vision:` block (with `if hasattr(model, "config"):`).

**`__all__` in save.py (line 48-54):**
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

**LoRA weight extraction (from `get_lora_parameters_bias` in kernels/utils.py:235):**
```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), proj.lora_A[adapter].weight, proj.lora_B[adapter].weight, proj.scaling[adapter], base_layer.bias)
```
Returns: (W, quant_state, A_weight, B_weight, scaling, bias)
- `scaling` = alpha/rank
- A shape: (rank, in_features), B shape: (out_features, rank)

**How `_merge_lora()` works (save.py:159):**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        # dequantize if 4-bit...
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        if A is not None:
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
```

**GGUF export signature (save.py:1768):**
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion: str = None,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    ...
):
```

**Imports at top of save.py:**
```python
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from typing import Optional, Callable, Union, List
import torch, os, shutil, gc
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
```

**PEFT config access:**
- `model.peft_config` is a dict of adapter configs
- Each config has: `r` (rank), `lora_alpha`, `target_modules`, `lora_dropout`, `bias`
- The active adapter name is typically "default"

### Requirements:
1. Add an `export_to_onnx` method (as a function in save.py + register in `patch_saving_functions`)
2. The method should:
   - Extract LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) — NOT merge them
   - Create an ONNX-compatible representation of the LoRA adapter
   - Save to a specified output directory
   - Include metadata (rank, alpha, target modules)
3. Parameters: `output_dir`, `opset_version` (default 17)
4. Add docstrings and type hints
5. Register/expose alongside existing save methods

### Design Considerations:
- This exports just the LoRA adapter as ONNX (not the full model) — so we iterate LoRA layers, extract A and B weight tensors, and create ONNX graph nodes
- The ONNX graph should represent the LoRA computation: output = x @ A^T @ B^T * scaling
- We should save metadata (rank, alpha, target_modules) in ONNX model metadata_props
- Use `onnx` Python package (optional import, raise helpful error if not installed)
- Follow the existing pattern: define function, register via `types.MethodType` in `patch_saving_functions`

Please create a detailed step-by-step implementation plan covering:

2. The function signature and implementation approach
3. How to extract LoRA weights without merging
4. How to build the ONNX representation
5. How to register the new method
6. What to add to __all__

content.text:
I now have a complete understanding of the codebase. Let me produce the detailed implementation plan.

---

## Detailed Implementation Plan: ONNX Export for LoRA Adapters

### Overview

This plan adds an `export_to_onnx` method to unsloth models that exports the LoRA adapter (A and B weight matrices, plus scaling metadata) as an ONNX model -- without merging the weights into the base model. The ONNX graph represents the LoRA computation: `output = x @ A^T @ B^T * scaling` for each target module.

Only one file requires substantive changes: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/save.py`. No other files need modification.

---

### Step 1: Add the `onnx` optional import guard (save.py, near top of file, around line 46)

Insert a lazy-import helper pattern near the existing imports. This follows the same pattern the codebase uses elsewhere (e.g., the `try/except` for `get_token` at lines 36-44). The actual import will be done inside the function body, but we define a helper to give a clear error message:

```python
def _check_onnx_installed():
    try:
        import onnx
        return onnx
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
            "Install it via: pip install onnx"
        )
```

Place this helper function after the `__all__` declaration (after line 54) and before the `LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS` constant at line 57.

---

### Step 2: Add `"unsloth_export_to_onnx"` to `__all__` (save.py, line 48-54)

Change the `__all__` list from:

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

to:

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "unsloth_export_to_onnx",
]
```

No changes needed in `unsloth/__init__.py` since it already does `from .save import *` at line 263 and will automatically pick up anything in `__all__`.

---

### Step 3: Define the `unsloth_export_to_onnx` function (save.py, insert before `patch_saving_functions` at line 2569)

A good insertion point is around line 2562, right after the `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` function ends and before `not_implemented_save` at line 2564. This keeps it grouped with the other export/save functions.

#### Function signature:

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def unsloth_export_to_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir      : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version   : int = 17,
) -> str:
```

**Parameters:**
- `self` -- the model instance (will be bound via `types.MethodType`)
- `output_dir` -- directory path where the ONNX file and metadata JSON will be saved
- `opset_version` -- ONNX opset version (default 17, which supports all needed ops: MatMul, Mul, Add)

**Returns:** the path to the saved `.onnx` file as a string.

#### Docstring:

```python
"""
Export LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format without merging into the base model.

Creates an ONNX model representing the LoRA computation for each target module:
    output = x @ A^T @ B^T * scaling

The adapter's rank, alpha, scaling factor, and target module names are stored
as ONNX model metadata_props.

Args:
    output_dir: Directory where the ONNX file will be saved. Created if it does not exist.
    opset_version: ONNX opset version to use. Default is 17.

Returns:
    Path to the saved ONNX model file.

Raises:
    ImportError: If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.
    ValueError: If no LoRA adapters are found on the model.
"""
```

#### Implementation logic (pseudocode with key details):

**3a. Check onnx is installed:**
```python
onnx = _check_onnx_installed()
from onnx import helper, TensorProto, numpy_helper
import numpy as np
```

**3b. Extract the active adapter name and peft config:**
```python
# Get active adapter name - follows the pattern from kernels/utils.py lines 245-246
adapter_name = getattr(self, "active_adapters", None)
if adapter_name is None:
    adapter_name = getattr(self, "active_adapter", ("default",))
adapter_name = adapter_name[0] if isinstance(adapter_name, (list, tuple)) else adapter_name

# Get peft config for metadata
peft_config = self.peft_config[adapter_name]
rank = peft_config.r
lora_alpha = peft_config.lora_alpha
target_modules = list(peft_config.target_modules)
```

**3c. Iterate all named modules, find LoRA layers, extract A and B weights:**

Use `model.named_modules()` (as done throughout the codebase, e.g., in `unsloth/models/llama.py:2249`, `unsloth/models/granite.py:517`) and check for `isinstance(module, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear))` (same guard as `_merge_lora` at line 162).

For each LoRA layer, call `get_lora_parameters_bias(module)` to extract `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)`. If `A is None`, the layer has no active LoRA adapter, so skip it.

```python
lora_layers = {}  # Maps module_name -> (A_weight, B_weight, scaling)

for name, module in self.named_modules():
    if not isinstance(module, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        continue
    
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(module)
    if A is None:
        continue  # No active LoRA adapter on this layer
    
    # A shape: (rank, in_features)
    # B shape: (out_features, rank)
    # s: scalar scaling = alpha / rank
    lora_layers[name] = (
        A.detach().cpu().float().numpy(),
        B.detach().cpu().float().numpy(),
        float(s),
    )

if len(lora_layers) == 0:
    raise ValueError("Unsloth: No LoRA adapters found on the model. Cannot export to ONNX.")
```

**3d. Build the ONNX graph:**

The ONNX graph represents a LoRA adapter module that takes input activations and returns the LoRA delta for each target layer. For each LoRA layer, the subgraph computes: `delta = (x @ A^T) @ B^T * scaling`.

The structure: one ONNX graph with a separate set of nodes per LoRA layer. Each layer gets:
- Two initializer tensors: `{name}.lora_A` and `{name}.lora_B`
- A scalar constant: `{name}.scaling`
- One input: `{name}.input` (shape: [batch, seq_len, in_features])
- One output: `{name}.output` (shape: [batch, seq_len, out_features])
- Three operations: MatMul(input, A^T) -> MatMul(result, B^T) -> Mul(result, scaling)

```python
initializers = []
nodes = []
inputs = []
outputs = []

for layer_name, (A_np, B_np, scaling) in lora_layers.items():
    # Sanitize the layer name for ONNX node naming (replace '.' with '/')
    safe_name = layer_name.replace(".", "/")
    
    rank, in_features = A_np.shape
    out_features, _ = B_np.shape
    
    # Create initializer tensors for A^T and B^T (pre-transposed for MatMul)
    A_T = A_np.T  # Shape: (in_features, rank)
    B_T = B_np.T  # Shape: (rank, out_features)
    
    init_A = numpy_helper.from_array(A_T.astype(np.float32), name=f"{safe_name}/lora_A_T")
    init_B = numpy_helper.from_array(B_T.astype(np.float32), name=f"{safe_name}/lora_B_T")
    init_s = numpy_helper.from_array(
        np.array(scaling, dtype=np.float32), name=f"{safe_name}/scaling"
    )
    initializers.extend([init_A, init_B, init_s])
    
    # Input for this layer
    inp = helper.make_tensor_value_info(
        f"{safe_name}/input", TensorProto.FLOAT, [None, None, in_features]
    )
    inputs.append(inp)
    
    # Nodes: x @ A^T -> intermediate @ B^T -> result * scaling
    node_matmul_A = helper.make_node(
        "MatMul",
        inputs=[f"{safe_name}/input", f"{safe_name}/lora_A_T"],
        outputs=[f"{safe_name}/after_A"],
        name=f"{safe_name}/matmul_A",
    )
    node_matmul_B = helper.make_node(
        "MatMul",
        inputs=[f"{safe_name}/after_A", f"{safe_name}/lora_B_T"],
        outputs=[f"{safe_name}/after_B"],
        name=f"{safe_name}/matmul_B",
    )
    node_scale = helper.make_node(
        "Mul",
        inputs=[f"{safe_name}/after_B", f"{safe_name}/scaling"],
        outputs=[f"{safe_name}/output"],
        name=f"{safe_name}/scale",
    )
    nodes.extend([node_matmul_A, node_matmul_B, node_scale])
    
    # Output for this layer
    out = helper.make_tensor_value_info(
        f"{safe_name}/output", TensorProto.FLOAT, [None, None, out_features]
    )
    outputs.append(out)
```

**3e. Assemble the ONNX model with metadata:**

```python
graph = helper.make_graph(
    nodes,
    "lora_adapter",
    inputs,
    outputs,
    initializer=initializers,
)

model_proto = helper.make_model(graph, opset_imports=[helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)])
model_proto.ir_version = 8  # Compatible with opset 17

# Add metadata properties
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="lora_rank", value=str(rank))
)
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="lora_alpha", value=str(lora_alpha))
)
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="target_modules", value=",".join(target_modules))
)
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="adapter_name", value=adapter_name)
)
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="num_lora_layers", value=str(len(lora_layers)))
)
model_proto.metadata_props.append(
    onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="exported_by", value="unsloth")
)

# Validate the model
onnx.checker.check_model(model_proto)
```

**3f. Save the ONNX model to disk:**

```python
os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_adapter.onnx")
onnx.save(model_proto, output_path)

print(f"Unsloth: LoRA adapter exported to ONNX at {output_path}")
print(f"  Adapter: {adapter_name}, Rank: {rank}, Alpha: {lora_alpha}")
print(f"  Target modules: {target_modules}")
print(f"  Number of LoRA layers exported: {len(lora_layers)}")

return output_path
```

---

### Step 4: Register the method in `patch_saving_functions` (save.py, inside `patch_saving_functions` starting at line 2569)

Add the registration in two places:

**4a. In the `if not vision:` block (line 2658), inside the `if hasattr(model, "config"):` guard (line 2659), add after line 2667:**

```python
model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)
```

This goes right after the `model.save_pretrained_ggml` assignment at line 2667.

**4b. In the `else:` block for vision models (line 2669), add after line 2675:**

```python
model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)
```

The ONNX export operates generically on LoRA layers via `named_modules()` and `get_lora_parameters_bias()`, so it works for both text and vision models without needing any model-architecture-specific code like `LLAMA_WEIGHTS`. This is a key advantage over the GGUF export path which is architecture-specific.

---

### Step 5: Summary of all changes by file

**File: `unsloth/save.py`**

| Location | Change |
|---|---|
| Lines 48-54 (`__all__`) | Add `"unsloth_export_to_onnx"` to the list |
| After line 54, before line 57 | Add `_check_onnx_installed()` helper function |
| After line 2561 (after `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` ends) | Add the `unsloth_export_to_onnx` function (~100-120 lines) |
| Line 2667 (inside `if not vision: if hasattr(model, "config"):`) | Add `model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)` |
| Line 2675 (inside `else:` vision block) | Add `model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)` |

**No other files need changes.** The `unsloth/__init__.py` already does `from .save import *` which will pick up the new export from the updated `__all__`.

---

### Design Decisions and Rationale

1. **Why store A^T and B^T (pre-transposed) as initializers instead of A and B with Transpose nodes?** Fewer ONNX nodes means simpler graph and faster inference. The ONNX MatMul op computes `input @ weight`, and since `get_lora_parameters_bias` returns A with shape `(rank, in_features)`, we need `x @ A^T` which is `x @ A.T`. Pre-transposing avoids runtime Transpose operations.

2. **Why one graph with per-layer I/O rather than separate ONNX models per layer?** A single file is easier to distribute and load. The per-layer inputs/outputs allow runtime frameworks to feed activations from the appropriate base model layer. Naming them with the layer path (e.g., `model/layers/0/self_attn/q_proj/input`) provides a clear mapping back to the base model.

3. **Why use `named_modules()` iteration instead of `LLAMA_WEIGHTS`?** The `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` constant is architecture-specific (llama only). Using `named_modules()` with `isinstance` checks works for all architectures (llama, gemma, granite, vision models). This is the same pattern used in `_merge_lora()` -- the `isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear))` check at line 162.

4. **Why use `get_lora_parameters_bias` instead of accessing `lora_A`/`lora_B` directly?** This function already handles edge cases: finding the active adapter, dealing with disabled/merged adapters, and extracting the correct scaling factor. Reusing it avoids duplicating that logic.

5. **Why `.` to `/` name sanitization?** ONNX node names with dots can confuse some ONNX tools. Using `/` is the conventional separator in ONNX graphs and avoids issues with downstream parsers.

6. **Why not include `torch.onnx.export`?** `torch.onnx.export` traces an entire `nn.Module` forward pass. Here we are only exporting the LoRA delta computation (not the base model), so building the ONNX graph manually with the `onnx` helper API is more direct and avoids needing the base model weights or a forward pass trace.

---

### Potential Challenges

1. **Large number of LoRA layers**: A 70B model with LoRA on all attention+MLP projections across 80 layers = 560 LoRA layers. The ONNX file could be large. This is acceptable since it is fundamentally just the adapter weights, but we should print the file size after saving.

2. **Mixed-rank LoRA**: Some configurations use different ranks for different modules. The current approach handles this naturally since we extract per-layer, but the metadata `lora_rank` would only reflect the config's single `r` value. Consider adding per-layer rank info as additional metadata entries or a JSON sidecar if needed. For V1, the single config rank is sufficient since unsloth enforces a uniform rank per adapter.

3. **Multiple active adapters**: The current implementation only exports the first active adapter (`adapter_name[0]`). This matches how the rest of unsloth handles adapters. Supporting multi-adapter export could be a future enhancement.

4. **`onnx` package not installed**: The lazy import with a clear error message in `_check_onnx_installed()` ensures a good user experience. The `onnx` package should NOT be added as a hard dependency in setup.py/requirements -- it is optional.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/save.py` -- The primary file. All new code goes here: the `_check_onnx_installed` helper, the `unsloth_export_to_onnx` function, the `__all__` update, and the `patch_saving_functions` registration.
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` -- Contains `get_lora_parameters_bias()` at line 235, which is the key dependency for extracting LoRA weights. Not modified, but must be understood.
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/__init__.py` -- Does `from .save import *` at line 263. Not modified, but confirms automatic re-export via `__all__`.
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/models/llama.py` -- Calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` at lines 2204 and 2902. Not modified, but confirms the registration mechanism works for text models.
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395426543-2rjl12/unsloth/models/vision.py` -- Calls `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=True)` at lines 702, 918, 964. Not modified, but confirms vision model registration path.

input.content:
# Plan: Add ONNX Export Support for LoRA Adapters in Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth currently supports saving LoRA adapters in PEFT format, merged HuggingFace format, GGUF, and GGML — but has **no ONNX support** (confirmed: zero "onnx" references in the codebase). ONNX export of LoRA adapters would enable deployment to ONNX Runtime and other ONNX-compatible inference engines. This feature exports the **adapter weights only** (A and B matrices + scaling), not the full merged model, making it lightweight and composable.

## Files to Modify

**Only one file needs changes:** `unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines)

Supporting files (read-only, not modified):
- `unsloth/kernels/utils.py:235` — `get_lora_parameters_bias()` extracts (W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)
- `unsloth/__init__.py:263` — does `from .save import *`, auto-exports anything in `__all__`
- `unsloth/models/llama.py:2204,2902` — calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` for text models
- `unsloth/models/vision.py:702,918,964` — calls `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=True)`

## Changes to `unsloth/save.py`

### Change 1: Update `__all__` (line 48–54)

Add `"unsloth_export_to_onnx"` to the `__all__` list so it's re-exported via `__init__.py`.

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "unsloth_export_to_onnx",
]
```

### Change 2: Add `_check_onnx_installed()` helper (after line 54, before line 56)

Lazy import guard — `onnx` is an **optional** dependency (not added to requirements):

```python
def _check_onnx_installed():
    """Check if the onnx package is installed and return it."""
    try:
        import onnx
        return onnx
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
            "Install it via: pip install onnx"
        )
```

### Change 3: Add `unsloth_export_to_onnx()` function (after line 2561, before `not_implemented_save` at line 2564)

Insert between the end of `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` and `not_implemented_save`.

#### Signature and docstring:

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def unsloth_export_to_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> str:
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.

    Creates an ONNX model representing the LoRA delta computation for each
    adapted layer: ``delta = x @ A^T @ B^T * scaling``. The adapter's rank,
    alpha, scaling factor, and target module names are stored as ONNX metadata.

    Only the adapter weights (A and B matrices) are exported — the base model
    weights are not included.

    Args:
        output_dir: Directory where the ONNX file will be saved. Created if needed.
        opset_version: ONNX opset version. Default ``17``.

    Returns:
        Path to the saved ``lora_adapter.onnx`` file.

    Raises:
        ImportError: If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.
        ValueError: If no LoRA adapters are found on the model.
    """
```

#### Implementation logic (5 steps):

**Step A — Import onnx:**
```python
onnx = _check_onnx_installed()
from onnx import helper, TensorProto, numpy_helper
import numpy as np
```

**Step B — Extract adapter config metadata:**
Access `self.peft_config` (dict keyed by adapter name). Get active adapter name using same pattern as `get_lora_parameters_bias` in `kernels/utils.py:245-247`:
```python
adapter_name = getattr(self, "active_adapters", getattr(self, "active_adapter", ("default",)))
adapter_name = adapter_name[0] if isinstance(adapter_name, (list, tuple)) else adapter_name
peft_config = self.peft_config[adapter_name]
rank = peft_config.r
lora_alpha = peft_config.lora_alpha
target_modules = sorted(peft_config.target_modules)
```

**Step C — Iterate named_modules, extract LoRA weights via `get_lora_parameters_bias`:**

Reuse the same `isinstance` guard as `_merge_lora` (save.py:162):
```python
lora_layers = {}
for name, module in self.named_modules():
    if not isinstance(module, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        continue
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(module)
    if A is None:
        continue
    lora_layers[name] = (
        A.detach().cpu().float().numpy(),   # shape: (rank, in_features)
        B.detach().cpu().float().numpy(),   # shape: (out_features, rank)
        float(s),                            # scaling = alpha / rank
    )

if not lora_layers:
    raise ValueError("Unsloth: No LoRA adapters found on the model.")
```

**Step D — Build ONNX graph:**

For each LoRA layer, create:
- Initializers: `lora_A_T` (pre-transposed, shape `in_features × rank`) and `lora_B_T` (`rank × out_features`) and `scaling` (scalar)
- Input: `{name}/input` with shape `[None, None, in_features]`
- Nodes: `MatMul(input, A_T)` → `MatMul(result, B_T)` → `Mul(result, scaling)`
- Output: `{name}/output` with shape `[None, None, out_features]`

Sanitize names: replace `.` with `/` (ONNX convention).

Assemble graph → model with `helper.make_model(graph, opset_imports=[...])`.

Add metadata_props:
- `lora_rank`, `lora_alpha`, `target_modules` (comma-separated), `adapter_name`, `num_lora_layers`, `exported_by=unsloth`

Validate with `onnx.checker.check_model()`.

**Step E — Save to disk:**
```python
os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_adapter.onnx")
onnx.save(model_proto, output_path)
print(f"Unsloth: LoRA adapter exported to ONNX at {output_path}")
print(f"  Adapter: {adapter_name}, Rank: {rank}, Alpha: {lora_alpha}")
print(f"  Target modules: {target_modules}")
print(f"  LoRA layers exported: {len(lora_layers)}")
return output_path
```

### Change 4: Register in `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569)

Add `model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)` in **two places**:

**4a. Non-vision models** — after line 2667 (inside `if not vision: if hasattr(model, "config"):`):
```python
model.export_to_onnx         = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx,  model)
```

**4b. Vision models** — after line 2675 (inside the `else:` block):
```python
model.export_to_onnx         = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx,  model)
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Export adapter only, not merged model** — ONNX representation of the LoRA delta is lightweight and composable with any base model inference
2. **Use `get_lora_parameters_bias`** (from `kernels/utils.py`) — reuses existing LoRA extraction logic that handles active adapter resolution, disabled/merged adapter detection
3. **Use `named_modules()` with `isinstance` check** — architecture-agnostic (works for llama, gemma, granite, vision, etc.), unlike `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` which is llama-specific
4. **Build ONNX graph manually** (not `torch.onnx.export`) — we only export the adapter delta computation, not a full model forward pass; manual graph construction is cleaner
5. **Pre-transpose A and B in initializers** — avoids runtime Transpose nodes in the ONNX graph
6. **`onnx` is optional** — lazy import with clear error message; NOT added to hard dependencies

## Verification

After implementation, verify with:

1. **Syntax check**: `python -c "import unsloth.save"` (checks no import errors)
2. **Function exists in exports**: `python -c "from unsloth.save import unsloth_export_to_onnx; print('OK')"` 
3. **Unit test** (requires GPU + model, so manual):
   ```python
   from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
   model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained("unsloth/tinyllama-bnb-4bit", ...)
   model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, r=16, target_modules=["q_proj","v_proj"])
   path = model.export_to_onnx("./test_onnx_output")
   # Verify file exists and loads
   import onnx
   m = onnx.load(path)
   onnx.checker.check_model(m)
   print({p.key: p.value for p in m.metadata_props})
   ```
4. **Check the ONNX graph structure**: verify each LoRA layer has MatMul→MatMul→Mul nodes, correct tensor shapes, and metadata contains rank/alpha/target_modules

# Plan: Add ONNX Export Support for LoRA Adapters in Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth currently supports saving LoRA adapters in PEFT format, merged HuggingFace format, GGUF, and GGML — but has **no ONNX support** (confirmed: zero "onnx" references in the codebase). ONNX export of LoRA adapters would enable deployment to ONNX Runtime and other ONNX-compatible inference engines. This feature exports the **adapter weights only** (A and B matrices + scaling), not the full merged model, making it lightweight and composable.

## Files to Modify

**Only one file needs changes:** `unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines)

Supporting files (read-only, not modified):
- `unsloth/kernels/utils.py:235` — `get_lora_parameters_bias()` extracts (W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)
- `unsloth/__init__.py:263` — does `from .save import *`, auto-exports anything in `__all__`
- `unsloth/models/llama.py:2204,2902` — calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` for text models
- `unsloth/models/vision.py:702,918,964` — calls `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=True)`

## Changes to `unsloth/save.py`

### Change 1: Update `__all__` (line 48–54)

Add `"unsloth_export_to_onnx"` to the `__all__` list so it's re-exported via `__init__.py`.

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "unsloth_export_to_onnx",
]
```

### Change 2: Add `_check_onnx_installed()` helper (after line 54, before line 56)

Lazy import guard — `onnx` is an **optional** dependency (not added to requirements):

```python
def _check_onnx_installed():
    """Check if the onnx package is installed and return it."""
    try:
        import onnx
        return onnx
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
            "Install it via: pip install onnx"
        )
```

### Change 3: Add `unsloth_export_to_onnx()` function (after line 2561, before `not_implemented_save` at line 2564)

Insert between the end of `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` and `not_implemented_save`.

#### Signature and docstring:

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def unsloth_export_to_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> str:
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.

    Creates an ONNX model representing the LoRA delta computation for each
    adapted layer: ``delta = x @ A^T @ B^T * scaling``. The adapter's rank,
    alpha, scaling factor, and target module names are stored as ONNX metadata.

    Only the adapter weights (A and B matrices) are exported — the base model
    weights are not included.

    Args:
        output_dir: Directory where the ONNX file will be saved. Created if needed.
        opset_version: ONNX opset version. Default ``17``.

    Returns:
        Path to the saved ``lora_adapter.onnx`` file.

    Raises:
        ImportError: If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.
        ValueError: If no LoRA adapters are found on the model.
    """
```

#### Implementation logic (5 steps):

**Step A — Import onnx:**
```python
onnx = _check_onnx_installed()
from onnx import helper, TensorProto, numpy_helper
import numpy as np
```

**Step B — Extract adapter config metadata:**
Access `self.peft_config` (dict keyed by adapter name). Get active adapter name using same pattern as `get_lora_parameters_bias` in `kernels/utils.py:245-247`:
```python
adapter_name = getattr(self, "active_adapters", getattr(self, "active_adapter", ("default",)))
adapter_name = adapter_name[0] if isinstance(adapter_name, (list, tuple)) else adapter_name
peft_config = self.peft_config[adapter_name]
rank = peft_config.r
lora_alpha = peft_config.lora_alpha
target_modules = sorted(peft_config.target_modules)
```

**Step C — Iterate named_modules, extract LoRA weights via `get_lora_parameters_bias`:**

Reuse the same `isinstance` guard as `_merge_lora` (save.py:162):
```python
lora_layers = {}
for name, module in self.named_modules():
    if not isinstance(module, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        continue
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(module)
    if A is None:
        continue
    lora_layers[name] = (
        A.detach().cpu().float().numpy(),   # shape: (rank, in_features)
        B.detach().cpu().float().numpy(),   # shape: (out_features, rank)
        float(s),                            # scaling = alpha / rank
    )

if not lora_layers:
    raise ValueError("Unsloth: No LoRA adapters found on the model.")
```

**Step D — Build ONNX graph:**

For each LoRA layer, create:
- Initializers: `lora_A_T` (pre-transposed, shape `in_features × rank`) and `lora_B_T` (`rank × out_features`) and `scaling` (scalar)
- Input: `{name}/input` with shape `[None, None, in_features]`
- Nodes: `MatMul(input, A_T)` → `MatMul(result, B_T)` → `Mul(result, scaling)`
- Output: `{name}/output` with shape `[None, None, out_features]`

Sanitize names: replace `.` with `/` (ONNX convention).

Assemble graph → model with `helper.make_model(graph, opset_imports=[...])`.

Add metadata_props:
- `lora_rank`, `lora_alpha`, `target_modules` (comma-separated), `adapter_name`, `num_lora_layers`, `exported_by=unsloth`

Validate with `onnx.checker.check_model()`.

**Step E — Save to disk:**
```python
os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_adapter.onnx")
onnx.save(model_proto, output_path)
print(f"Unsloth: LoRA adapter exported to ONNX at {output_path}")
print(f"  Adapter: {adapter_name}, Rank: {rank}, Alpha: {lora_alpha}")
print(f"  Target modules: {target_modules}")
print(f"  LoRA layers exported: {len(lora_layers)}")
return output_path
```

### Change 4: Register in `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569)

Add `model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)` in **two places**:

**4a. Non-vision models** — after line 2667 (inside `if not vision: if hasattr(model, "config"):`):
```python
model.export_to_onnx         = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx,  model)
```

**4b. Vision models** — after line 2675 (inside the `else:` block):
```python
model.export_to_onnx         = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx,  model)
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Export adapter only, not merged model** — ONNX representation of the LoRA delta is lightweight and composable with any base model inference
2. **Use `get_lora_parameters_bias`** (from `kernels/utils.py`) — reuses existing LoRA extraction logic that handles active adapter resolution, disabled/merged adapter detection
3. **Use `named_modules()` with `isinstance` check** — architecture-agnostic (works for llama, gemma, granite, vision, etc.), unlike `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` which is llama-specific
4. **Build ONNX graph manually** (not `torch.onnx.export`) — we only export the adapter delta computation, not a full model forward pass; manual graph construction is cleaner
5. **Pre-transpose A and B in initializers** — avoids runtime Transpose nodes in the ONNX graph
6. **`onnx` is optional** — lazy import with clear error message; NOT added to hard dependencies

## Verification

After implementation, verify with:

1. **Syntax check**: `python -c "import unsloth.save"` (checks no import errors)
2. **Function exists in exports**: `python -c "from unsloth.save import unsloth_export_to_onnx; print('OK')"` 
3. **Unit test** (requires GPU + model, so manual):
   ```python
   from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
   model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained("unsloth/tinyllama-bnb-4bit", ...)
   model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, r=16, target_modules=["q_proj","v_proj"])
   path = model.export_to_onnx("./test_onnx_output")
   # Verify file exists and loads
   import onnx
   m = onnx.load(path)
   onnx.checker.check_model(m)
   print({p.key: p.value for p in m.metadata_props})
   ```
4. **Check the ONNX graph structure**: verify each LoRA layer has MatMul→MatMul→Mul nodes, correct tensor shapes, and metadata contains rank/alpha/target_modules

Here's a summary of the plan:

**Single file to modify:** `unsloth/save.py` — 4 surgical changes:

1. **`__all__`** (line 48): Add `"unsloth_export_to_onnx"` to exports
2. **`_check_onnx_installed()`** (after line 54): Lazy import helper with clear error if `onnx` isn't installed (optional dependency)
3. **`unsloth_export_to_onnx()`** (~100 lines, inserted after line 2561): The main function that:
   - Extracts adapter config (rank, alpha, target_modules) from `self.peft_config`
   - Iterates `self.named_modules()`, uses existing `get_lora_parameters_bias()` to extract A/B matrices + scaling per LoRA layer
   - Builds an ONNX graph with `MatMul→MatMul→Mul` nodes per layer (representing `delta = x @ A^T @ B^T * scaling`)
   - Stores metadata as ONNX model properties
   - Saves to `{output_dir}/lora_adapter.onnx`
4. **`patch_saving_functions()`** (lines 2667, 2675): Register `model.export_to_onnx` via `types.MethodType` for both text and vision models

The approach reuses existing infrastructure (`get_lora_parameters_bias`, `isinstance` checks, `types.MethodType` registration) and works across all model architectures without architecture-specific code.

        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False
    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
    created_folder = False
    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
        created_folder = True
        os.makedirs(file_location)
    pass
    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
        sentencepiece_model = True
    pass
    if created_folder:
        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
    return sentencepiece_model

def _free_cached_model(model):
    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)

    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
            )

            delete_strategy.execute()
        pass
    pass
pass


def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
